commands.review_summary:
This PR adds `messages.tts.providers.volcengine.allowPrivateNetwork` and forwards it into both Volcengine Seed Speech and legacy TTS guarded HTTP calls as fake-IP-only SSRF policy flags.

PR surface: Source +30. Total +30 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current Volcengine TTS calls only pass a hostname allowlist, while the SSRF layer blocks 198.18/15 and ULA by default unless the narrow policy bits are set. A live TUN proxy after-fix proof is still missing.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor real behavior proof plus maintainer review of the security/config contract; automation cannot supply the external TUN proxy evidence.

target.title: feat(volcengine): add allowPrivateNetwork option for TTS behind TUN proxy
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds `allowPrivateNetwork` boolean option (default `false`) to Volcengine TTS provider
- When enabled, passes `allowPrivateNetwork: true` to the SSRF guard in both Seed Speech and legacy TTS HTTP calls
- Configurable via `messages.tts.providers.volcengine.allowPrivateNetwork` in openclaw.json or programmatic config

## Motivation
When running behind a TUN proxy (e.g., Surge, Clash) that intercepts `198.18.x.x` addresses for fast DNS resolution, the built-in SSRF guard `fetchWithSsrFGuard` blocks these RFC 1918 addresses. This makes TTS fail silently. The flag lets users behind such proxies explicitly opt in to private-network connections.

## Changes
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `extensions/volcengine/tts.ts` | Add `allowPrivateNetwork` param to type + destructure in both TTS functions; conditional spread in `policy` |
| `extensions/volcengine/speech-provider.ts` | Read flag from raw config, pass through `readProviderConfig`, forward to `volcengineTTS()` call |

## Test plan
- [ ] Default (no flag): TTS works as before, SSRF guard blocks private IPs
- [ ] `allowPrivateNetwork: true`: TTS succeeds behind TUN proxy intercepting 198.18.x.x
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: geekhuashan
